page: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-4 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a base clock speed of 3.1 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, the processor includes a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 24 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU accelerators, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate moderate single-core capabilities alongside constrained multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves average tier results in stress-ng single-core CPU testing with a score of 1572.39 and Geekbench single-core scoring at 1309.0. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression at 26.8 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 10.09 GB/sec also perform in the middle tier. However, multi-core execution falls into the lower percentiles, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 2855.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 4761.94. Memory bandwidth is similarly limited, registering 28.26 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 24.11 GB/sec for large-block write operations.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation and 3.1 GHz clock speed, the c2-standard-4 is suited for compute-bound workloads that do not scale heavily across many cores, such as single-threaded processing, light web serving, and basic text processing. The lack of bundled local storage requires external block storage attachment, which may impact I/O-heavy operations. The instance exhibits weak performance in database operations with a score of 1545.89 on Passmark and Redis SET operations at 4.1 million operations per second, indicating it is less optimal for high-throughput transactional databases or dense multi-threaded compilation tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 3.1 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of RAM, a 24 MB L3 cache, and lacks bundled local storage or GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1309.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1572.39. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the lower percentiles, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 2855.0 and uncached read bandwidth of 28.26 GB/sec. This hardware profile is suited for single-threaded compute tasks, light web serving, and text processing, but presents performance tradeoffs for memory-intensive applications, multi-threaded compilations, or high-throughput database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering moderate single-core performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-4 features 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, optimized for single-threaded compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated compute instance featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors for single-threaded workloads requiring balanced memory allocation.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon processor
16.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Average single-core performance suitable for single-threaded tasks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for light web serving, text processing, and basic compute jobs
